what_it_does: Bash script that scrubs PII and project-specific identifiers from JSONL test fixtures in test/fixtures by applying a hardcoded list of sed substitutions and creating .bak backups.
observations: Bash script with shebang and set -e
Creates a .bak backup of each fixture before editing
Uses sed -i '' (macOS-style in-place edit with empty backup suffix)
Hardcoded substitutions cover local file paths, repository paths, git branches, project names, personal names, email addresses, and conversational phrases
Replaces tokens such as /Users/jesse/, dev.repeatably.com, Jesse, jesse@fsck.com, and branch names streaming/conversation-search
Ends with a prompt to review changes via git diff
standouts: The script embeds the exact PII/project tokens it is meant to remove
Includes phrase-level redactions like 'Looking at your instructions, Jesse' and 'Jesse's right'
Uses sed -i '' which is macOS-specific and not portable to GNU sed
conventions: Follows: shebang, set -e, relative directory usage, backup-before-mutate pattern, loop over files
Breaks: hardcodes real identifiers in source, uses platform-specific sed flag, leaves backup files alongside fixtures, has no dry-run or validation
concerns: Hardcoded real PII in the script itself
Global replacements like repeatably -> example may over-scrub non-sensitive occurrences
sed -i '' is macOS-specific and will fail on GNU sed
No handling for missing .jsonl files (unquoted glob expands literally)
Backup files are left in the fixtures directory and could be committed accidentally
Regex substitutions are brittle if fixture formatting or field order changes
notes: A quick-and-dirty fixture anonymizer that trades portability and robustness for a simple, explicit redaction list.
questions: Is this script meant to be run once manually or as part of CI?
Should the .bak files be gitignored or deleted after review?
Are the sed substitutions exhaustive enough for all current and future fixtures?
Why use sed -i '' instead of a portable in-place editing approach?
Could global replacement of 'repeatably'/'Repeatably' accidentally alter non-PII content?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches actual behavior: it scrubs PII/project info from JSONL fixtures using a fixed set of sed replacements and keeps backups; the implementation is manual and brittle rather than systematic.
intention_signals: The script is trying to remove identifying and project-specific details from recorded test fixtures so the sanitized data can be shared or committed without leaking personal or proprietary context.
novelty: It encodes the very PII it scrubs directly in the source and redacts conversational phrases, not just structured fields.
contribution: Provides an explicit, runnable anonymization recipe for fixture files, preserving data shape while replacing known sensitive tokens with generic placeholders; a fused system would keep the scrubbing ruleset and the backup safeguard.
summary_for_embedding: A bash script that iterates over test/fixtures/*.jsonl, backs each up as .bak, and applies a hardcoded set of sed substitutions to replace PII, project paths, repository names, git branches, names, emails, and conversational references with generic test values.
intention_for_embedding: Intended to sanitize test fixtures by removing project-specific and personally identifying information before sharing or committing them.
